# Project Instructions

## Overview

lazyman-ian marketplace for AI coding plugins. 5 plugins in a single repository.

| Plugin | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| dev-flow | Development workflow: brainstorm, plan, implement, review, commit, PR, release |
| ios-swift-plugin | iOS/Swift toolkit: SwiftUI, Concurrency, WidgetKit, Performance, Migration |
| android-kotlin-plugin | Android/Kotlin toolkit: Compose, Coroutines, Performance, Architecture |
| utils | Code quality: deslop, search-code, safety hooks |
| research | Research: Perplexity AI, Braintrust, RepoPrompt |

## Build Commands

```bash
npm install --prefix dev-flow/mcp-server
npm run --prefix dev-flow/mcp-server bundle    # Bundle MCP server (required)
npm run --prefix dev-flow/mcp-server build     # TypeScript compile
```

## Architecture

```
plugin-name/
  .claude-plugin/plugin.json    # Plugin manifest
  skills/                        # SKILL.md definitions
  agents/                        # Agent prompts (.md)
  hooks/                         # Hook configurations
```

## Code Standards

- Match existing file style (indentation, spacing, patterns)
- TODO/FIXME with ticket reference when possible
- Conventional commits: `type(scope): subject`
- Every task must have a verify command; success = exit code 0
- No AI attribution in commits

## Agents

| Agent | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| coroutine-reviewer | Review Kotlin code for coroutine anti-patterns, structured concurrency violations, and lifecycle-unsafe collection. |
| migration-scanner | Scan Android/Kotlin codebase for deprecated APIs, outdated libraries, and legacy patterns. |
| apply-agent | Apply agent that applies approved improvements to component files. |
| code-reviewer | Review code changes for quality, security, performance, and best practices with P0-P3 severity. |
| debug-agent | Debug issues by investigating logs, code, and git history. |
| diagnose-agent | Diagnosis agent that analyzes root causes of component performance issues. |
| evaluate-agent | Evaluation agent that analyzes session performance and identifies improvement targets. |
| implement-agent | Implementation agent that executes plan phases with TDD and creates handoffs. |
| plan-agent | Planning agent that creates implementation plans with research and iteration. |
| pr-describer | Generate comprehensive PR descriptions with bilingual support. |
| propose-agent | Proposal agent that generates improvement proposals for components. |
| skill-audit | Audits all skills across plugins for quality, triggering accuracy, line count compliance, allowed-tools coverage, and de |
| spec-reviewer | Verify implementation matches spec exactly — nothing more, nothing less. |
| validate-agent | Validation agent that checks plan tech choices against best practices. |
| verify-agent | Verification agent that verifies improvement effects after changes are applied. |
| concurrency-reviewer | Review Swift files for concurrency issues, Swift 6 compatibility, and thread-safety patterns. |
| migration-scanner | Scan Swift/iOS codebase for deprecated APIs, outdated libraries, and legacy patterns. |
| performance-auditor | Audit SwiftUI views for performance issues including view identity, expensive body operations, and rendering inefficienc |
| swift-testing-reviewer | Review Swift test files for Swift Testing anti-patterns, XCTest/Swift Testing mixing issues, and test isolation problems |
| braintrust-analyst | Analyze Claude Code sessions using Braintrust logs |
| repo-research-analyst | Analyze repository structure, patterns, conventions, and documentation |
| research-agent | Research agent for external documentation, best practices, and library APIs via MCP tools |

## Behavioral Rules

These rules map Claude Code hook behaviors to Codex. Follow them strictly.

### Shell Safety
- Use `builtin cd` for directory changes (zoxide alias)
- Use `/usr/bin/sed` instead of `sed` (sd alias)
- Use `/usr/bin/find` instead of `find` (fd alias)
- Use `/usr/bin/curl` instead of `curl` (xh alias)
- Use `npm run --prefix`, `git -C`, `make -C` instead of cd + command

### Commit Workflow
- NEVER use raw `git commit` — use `$dev-commit` skill or `dev_commit` MCP tool
- NEVER use `git push --force` — use `--force-with-lease`
- NEVER skip verification with `--no-verify`
- Before committing: search knowledge vault for relevant pitfalls

### Tool Usage
- Prefer Read over cat/head/tail
- Prefer Edit over sed/awk for file modification
- Prefer Glob over find for file search
- Prefer Grep over grep/rg for content search

### Session Discipline
- At session start: load active ledger from `thoughts/ledgers/`
- At session start: check `thoughts/knowledge/` for critical pitfalls
- After editing files: run the appropriate formatter
- If same command fails 3+ times: stop and try a different approach
- Every task must have a verify command; completion = exit code 0

### Code Quality
- Match existing file style (indentation, spacing, patterns)
- No AI attribution in commits
- Conventional commits: `type(scope): subject`
- P0/P1 review findings block commit

## Project Memory

> Auto-synced from Claude Code auto memory. Source of truth: `/Users/lazyman/.claude/projects/-Users-lazyman-work-claude-plugins/memory/MEMORY.md`

# Memory - claude-plugins

## Key Patterns

- HouseSigma repos: `~/work/HouseSigma/{housesigma-ios-native,housesigma-android-native,web-hybrid}`
- iOS/Android base: master, Web base: develop
- Branch: `feature/TASK-{id}-{name}`
- Marketplace: 5 plugins (dev-flow, ios-swift-plugin, android-kotlin-plugin, utils, research)

## Architecture Decisions

- **Knowledge Vault**: Markdown-first (`thoughts/knowledge/`) + SQLite FTS5 index, priority/decay scoring, quality gate
- **Review System**: code-reviewer agent in isolated context, P0-P3 severity, branch-scoped session log
- **5-Gate Pipeline**: Fresh Subagent → Self-Review → Spec Review → Quality Review → Batch Checkpoint
- **commit-guard.sh**: Hook 强制 `/dev commit`，matcher 用 `Bash(*git commit*)` 含 chained
- **Rules Distribution**: 12 templates via `/dev rules`, `paths:` frontmatter for platform-specific
- **Docs Scaffolding**: `/dev init` creates `docs/` with 5 templates + `@docs/` imports + 2 async hooks (docs-lint, docs-reminder)

## Lessons Learned

- `builtin` 只对 shell 内置命令生效 (`cd`, `echo`, `pwd`)，不能用于 `npm`, `node`, `git`
  - ✅ `npm run --prefix /path`, `git -C /path`, `make -C /path`
- Hook matcher `Bash(git commit*)` 只匹配开头，用 `Bash(*git commit*)` 匹配 chained
- Prompt rules 不足以阻止工作流绕过 — 需要 hook 强制执行
- 并行 agent 创建模式：research agents (4-6) → skill agents (7) → lead scaffolds
- Code review 发现跨平台术语泄漏 (iOS "NavigationStack" → Android 应为 "NavHost")
- **Prompt 瘦身 A/B 结论**: Rule 瘦身高 ROI (-48% lines, 0 质量损失)。Skill 瘦身视类型而定：探索型 (brainstorm) thin 反而更贵，结构型 (plan) thin 简单任务胜出但复杂任务持平
